2604.01094.title: Temporal Dependencies in In-Context Learning: The Role of Induction Heads
2604.01094.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong in-context learning capabilities, but how they track and retrieve information from context remains underexplored. Drawing on the free recall paradigm in cognitive science (where participants recall list items in any order), we show that several open-source LLMs consistently display a serial-recall-like pattern, assigning peak probability to tokens that immediately follow a repeated token in the input sequence. Through systematic ablation experiments, we show that induction heads, specialized attention heads that attend to the token following a previous occurrence of the current token, play an important role in this phenomenon. Removing heads with a high induction score substantially reduces the +1 lag bias, whereas ablating random heads does not reproduce the same reduction. We also show that removing heads with high induction scores impairs the performance of models prompted to do serial recall using few-shot learning to a larger extent than removing random heads. Our findings highlight a mechanistically specific connection between induction heads and temporal context processing in transformers, suggesting that these heads are especially important for ordered retrieval and serial-recall-like behavior during in-context learning.
2604.01094.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）展現出強大的上下文學習能力，但它們如何追蹤和檢索上下文中的資訊仍然未被充分探討。<lb/>基於認知科學中的自由回憶範式（參與者可以以任何順序回憶列表項目），我們顯示幾個開源LLM一致地顯示出類似序列回憶的模式，將最高概率分配給緊接在輸入序列中重複標記後的標記。<lb/>通過系統性的消融實驗，我們顯示誘導頭，專門關注當前標記之前出現的標記後的標記的注意力頭，在這一現象中扮演著重要角色。<lb/>移除具有高誘導分數的頭部顯著減少了+1滯後偏差，而隨機移除頭部則不會重現相同的減少效果。<lb/>我們還顯示，移除具有高誘導分數的頭部對於使用少量樣本學習進行序列回憶的模型性能影響更大，這比移除隨機頭部的影響更明顯。<lb/>我們的發現突顯了誘導頭與Transformer中的時間上下文處理之間的機制特定連結，表明這些頭對於有序檢索和上下文學習過程中的類序列回憶行為特別重要。
2604.01094.author: Anooshka Bajaj et.al.
2604.01094.authors: Anooshka Bajaj, Deven Mahesh Mistry, Sahaj Singh Maini, Yash Aggarwal, Billy Dickson, Zoran Tiganj
